appLayerLabels.description: Backend analyzer, storage, and runtime code.
Server-side API handler and protocol surface.
Contract data shared across product surfaces.
API schema, generated contract, or contract source.
Contract surface that is both API-owned and shared.
Frontend client code that talks to backend APIs.
Frontend unit, integration, or e2e test code.
Generated build or output files outside contract surfaces.
Process or community evidence spanning more than one App Layer.
Insufficient evidence for a stable App Layer.

semanticTerms.description: Primary product layer assigned to each graph node.
High-confidence functional ownership under an App Layer.
Server-facing API handlers and API protocol surface.
Schema or generated contract shared by API consumers.
Frontend client surface that calls backend APIs.
Persisted evidence that a source reference could not be resolved.
Unresolved target text retained without claiming a resolved in-repo symbol.
In-repo evidence that the analyzer could not resolve confidently.
Reference expected to resolve outside the indexed repository.
Builtin, standard library, fixture, or otherwise non-actionable unresolved reference.

functionalAreaLabels.description: Reference binding and unresolved-reference detection.
Topology health, diagnostic confidence, and graph quality policy.
Graph query, retrieval, and benchmark surfaces.
Model Context Protocol tools, resources, and integrations.
Frontend graph panels, filters, and visual controls.
Graph placement, rendering geometry, and layout controls.
Generated and shared cross-surface contracts.
Language provider extraction and ScopeIR conversion.
Runtime process, local server, and execution management.
Analyze pipeline phases and graph enrichment.
Interactive session, job, and chat runtime services.
Desktop launcher and startup flow.
Command-line entrypoints and command behavior.
Reports, ledgers, and problem records.
HTTP API and API client behavior.
Graph storage, LadybugDB export/load, and repository metadata.
Embedding generation, indexing, and semantic search support.
Project and build configuration.
Documentation content.
Process or community evidence spanning more than one Functional Area.
Insufficient evidence for a stable Functional Area.

factFamilies.evidence: definitions emit graph nodes and file DEFINES edges for provider-backed files
in-repo import targets emit IMPORTS and import-use USES edges; external imports remain unresolved evidence
resolved callable targets emit CALLS edges; unresolved/external calls remain metrics/evidence
resolved member/property accesses emit ACCESSES edges; unresolved accesses remain metrics/evidence
resolved type annotations and bindings emit USES edges; built-in/external types remain unresolved or intentionally ignored
owner/member definitions emit HAS_METHOD, HAS_PROPERTY, and MEMBER_OF-style graph relationships where supported
class extends facts resolve to EXTENDS plus compatibility INHERITS when in-repo targets are found
route/tool/process edges are framework/analyzer-derived and covered outside provider parity fixtures

factFamilies.evidence: COBOL PROGRAM-ID nodes and nesting relationships are emitted by internal/cobol
COBOL sections, paragraphs, PERFORM, and CALL metrics are emitted by internal/cobol
COPY statements are tracked by dedicated COBOL metrics and copybook expansion tests
JCL job/step program links are tracked by dedicated COBOL metrics and JCL tests
COBOL is scanned and processed by the dedicated analyzer phase; it is not routed through ScopeIR provider extraction
